limitations: Operator chat timestamps inside this slice are approximate unless anchored to repo receipts.
This log preserves the bounded plugin-ui reassessment and exact raw dev_deploy authority even though the client does not export exact per-message telemetry.

entries.speaker: operator
entries.message: The operator deliberately constrained the next versioned slice to a plugin UI reassessment so the lane could improve safely while current VR testing continued.

entries.speaker: assistant
entries.message: Planned the slice as a bounded operator test-surface pass: expose already-registered runtime readback in the plugin panel, surface the existing resize actions visibly, and avoid transport, scene-generator, or Meta-runtime interaction changes.

entries.speaker: assistant
entries.message: Built 0.6.14 cleanly, stamped the release artifacts, and produced a validated release boundary plus a build-only DevPlayer package report for the same version.

entries.speaker: assistant
entries.message: Redeployed the raw modern_tv hosted-player proof as the canonical 0.6.14 alpha dev_deploy seam, keeping the 2018 raw shell export authority and versioned preset, host asset, and plugin names aligned.

entries.speaker: assistant
entries.message: Refreshed the baseline direct-player raw asset from the validated 0.6.14 release output so the operator has both the generic player baseline and the modern_tv host proof on the same alpha core.

entries.speaker: assistant
entries.message: Refreshed the authored Volodeck witness packet so the current visual proof remains in lockstep with the raw 0.6.14 alpha while the operator tests in VR.

decisions: 0.6.14 is the next semantic version because the plugin UI and operator test surface changed after 0.6.13 had already been built and committed.
The live authority seam for this slice remains dev_deploy raw Custom artifacts, not the .var package.
This slice is intentionally bounded to plugin UI readback and visible resize controls and does not change playback transport logic.
The baseline direct-player raw asset and the modern_tv hosted proof must stay on the same 0.6.14 alpha player core.
Volodeck remains a visual witness seam and should be refreshed whenever the live raw alpha changes.
openQuestions: How does the 0.6.14 alpha plugin test surface feel in VR now that the panel exposes live readback and visible resize controls?
Which specific plugin UI groupings should be promoted next once the operator has live comfort feedback from the new panel shape?
When the operator provides the updated base scene layout, how much of the plugin UI should be mirrored or intentionally separated from the deterministic scene controls?
notes: The .var package report for 0.6.14 is build evidence only and remains undistributed.
This slice leaves the deeper Meta-runtime interaction lane intentionally untouched so the operator can test the current alpha safely.
